question: With a HEALTHY (semantic-similarity) incumbent instead of the collapsing lexical screen, does NLI entailment beat coherence/similarity as a grounding signal on sophia's domain?
incumbent: semantic-similarity (all-MiniLM cosine, threshold 0.45) — fires on topical evidence

realArm.label: REAL non-forge n=106; MDE ~0.21 -> UNDERPOWERED (point estimate interpretable now the incumbent is healthy, but CI wide)

forgeArm.label: FORGE paraphrase-expanded; INTERNAL-VALIDITY-ONLY (cases correlated -> CI optimistic, NOT an external result)

verdict: NOT GO. Incumbent-fix WORKED (semantic healthy, cov 0.78 vs lexical's collapsed 0.009). With an interpretable healthy incumbent: NLI beats semantic on F1-at-matched-coverage (real +0.053 CI [0.0,0.147] borderline/underpowered; forge +0.071 CI [0.058,0.140] internal-validity-only) — BUT by CATASTROPHIC over-abstention (NLI cov 0.057 vs 0.78; drop 0.73 >> 0.01 fails the calibrated-abstention guard). ROOT CAUSE: a CLAIM-EVIDENCE HEDGE MISMATCH — sophia's provenance evidence is epistemically hedged ('attributed to', 'compiled', 'legendary') so it does NOT strictly entail blunt 'X wrote Y' claims; NLI correctly abstains, semantic over-admits on topicality, lexical collapses. NLI abstaining here is CONSISTENT with sophia's own uncertainty discipline — the blunt claims are mis-specified for hedged evidence. sophia-domain qualifier NOT closed. Standing result: retrieval-bound GO holds only on FEVER (verbatim-ish evidence, blunt claims match). canClaimAGI=false.

threeWayCoverage_fixedHostnames.reading: lexical COLLAPSES (0.009 << 0.10, incumbent-degenerate — D's finding stands); semantic HEALTHY (0.78, over-admits on topicality); NLI OVER-ABSTAINS (0.057)

harnessBugFixed: sources_of shared hostname 'wiki.local' -> gate's >=2-independent-domains rule collapsed all evidence to 1 domain -> spurious coverage 0.0 in D. FIXED to distinct hostnames. D's lexical-collapse finding STANDS (true cov 0.0094, still << 0.10); the exact 0.0 was bug-inflated.

abstentionGuard.reading: NLI wins F1-at-matched-coverage only by admitting ~6% vs semantic's 78% — coverage drop 0.73 >> 0.01 FAILS the calibrated-abstention guard (the over-abstention pathology, at scale)
